_comment: Trait catalog — descriptive labels placed on cards alongside their aspect contribution. Mirrors aspects.json: top-level groups, each group holds `trait_name: description` string pairs. Trait IDs are 1-indexed in JSON insertion order across all groups (id 0 reserved as `TRAIT_NONE`). Append-only — once a trait name is assigned, never rename it; remove a trait by deleting its line and leaving a `_reserved_<name>` tombstone if downstream wire compatibility ever matters.

tile._comment: Traits whose meaning is scoped to tile cards.
tile.cost: Movement cost paid to enter the tile. Higher values represent rougher or slower terrain.
tile.height: Spatial elevation of the tile — affects lighting, reach, and terrain layering. Per-tile, not summed across a chain.

soul._comment: Traits whose meaning is scoped to soul cards.
soul.speed: Movement allowance per turn. Spent against tile `cost` when the soul traverses the map.

climate._comment: Climate-envelope axes read by the procedural world generator. Each axis (elevation, temperature, humidity, aether) is sampled per cell to a [0, 1] float; a tile is a procedural-placement candidate for a cell only when every axis's sample falls within that tile's [min, max] bound. Omitted bounds default to 0 (min) / 1 (max) — i.e. 'no constraint on this axis'. Today only tile cards declare envelopes; reusable for any entity that needs a climate-scoped existence (spawning fauna, weather events, ...).
climate.elevation_min: Lower bound (inclusive) of the elevation envelope. [0, 1]. Cells whose sampled elevation falls below this exclude the tile from the candidate set. Omitted ⇒ 0 (no lower bound).
climate.elevation_max: Upper bound (inclusive) of the elevation envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1 (no upper bound).
climate.temperature_min: Lower bound (inclusive) of the temperature envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = tundra, 1 = desert. Omitted ⇒ 0.
climate.temperature_max: Upper bound (inclusive) of the temperature envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.
climate.humidity_min: Lower bound (inclusive) of the humidity envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = arid, 1 = saturated. Omitted ⇒ 0.
climate.humidity_max: Upper bound (inclusive) of the humidity envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.
climate.aether_min: Lower bound (inclusive) of the aether-saturation envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = mundane, 1 = ley-line / resonance site. Omitted ⇒ 0.
climate.aether_max: Upper bound (inclusive) of the aether-saturation envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.

placement._comment: Procedural placement metadata. Read by the world generator AFTER the climate envelope selects candidate tiles for a cell — these traits decide which candidate wins and how it relates to its neighbors.
placement.rarity: Relative weight when multiple tiles share a cell's candidate set. Default 1.0 (uniform among candidates). Values < 1 make the tile uncommon, > 1 bias toward it. The generator weight-picks among candidates using rarity as the weight.
placement.coverage: Hex coverage role. 0 = decorator (overlay drawn on a base tile — trees, boulders, decorations); 1 = base tile (fills the hex). Read as boolean (`>= 0.5` is base). The generator's two-stage pass picks a base tile first, then rolls candidates with `coverage < 0.5` for an optional decorator overlay. Omitted ⇒ 1 (treat as base).
placement.cluster_group: Cluster group id — tiles sharing a non-zero value bias toward spawning adjacent to one another (forests clump; isolated boulders don't). Encoded as a small integer in f32 form (`as u8` on read). 0 = no clustering (default). Group ids are author-assigned; the generator only compares values for equality.
placement.cluster_strength: How much the cluster bias overrides the climate-only candidate roll. [0, 1]. 0 = clustering ignored (plain climate roll wins); 1 = if any neighbor shares this `cluster_group`, this tile wins regardless of climate-roll outcome. Intermediate values blend. Omitted ⇒ 0.
